text:
Once upon a time, there was a girl named Olive. She loved ducks. Every Saturday afternoon, Olive would wake up early and go out into the garden. There, among the vibrant green leaves, she'd feed the ducks. They were funny creatures.

One Sunday morning, Olive saw something odd. An enormous goose had fallen out of the sky. It was huge, like an elephant. Olive knew she had to help. She reached into her pocket and pulled out a tiny toy goose. She sewed that onto the big goose's feathers.

"Oh wow!" said the big goose. Suddenly, it flew up with the tiny goose tucked safely under its wing. Everyone admired them.

The big goose got lost quite often. One day, Olive noticed him wandering around a distant park. She flew there, clutching the tiny goose firmly.

"Theodore, my love!" she exclaimed.

The big goose recognized his favourite. He floated down, admiring Olive.

"I miss my favorite," he says.
